steps.title: Identify the right triangle from the diagram
steps.content: The diagram shows right triangle RST with the right angle at T. The hypotenuse RS = 53, and one leg (RT) is $\sqrt{105}$.

steps.title: Find the remaining leg using Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
With hypotenuse RS = 53 and leg RT = $\sqrt{105}$, apply the Pythagorean theorem to find the other leg ST:
$$(\sqrt{105})^2 + ST^2 = 53^2$$
$$105 + ST^2 = 2809$$
$$ST^2 = 2704$$
$$ST = 52$$

steps.title: Calculate sin R
steps.content:
In a right triangle, sine equals the ratio of opposite to hypotenuse. The side opposite angle R is ST = 52, and the hypotenuse is RS = 53:
$$\sin R = \frac{52}{53} \approx 0.981$$
